THEY ARE BOTH ASSOCIATES IN THE LAW FIRM OF MORRISON & FOERSTER FROM 1997 THROUGH 2005. HE WORKS IN ANOTHER DEPARTMENT OF THE FIRM. THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. ATTY. JUAL F. REYES OF KENNETH P. ROBERTS CORPORATION, TEL. NO. (818)888-3553 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AGILE I BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Walter Stella one of the attorneys for Defendant Corestaff. They are both associates in the Law firm of Morrison & Foerster from 1997 through 2005. He works in another department of the firm. This fact does not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CORPORATE EMPLOYMENT RESOURCES, INC's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint is granted. Defendant Corestaff's proposed cross-complaint presents claims arising from the same transaction, and is sought in good faith. See CCP 426.10(c), 426.50. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
